trigger_reason: R279 was originally classified UNCERTAIN (Phase-0 borderline at primary semantic 0.71). Under v10 step 13 retrofit, the candidate meets the FIRED criterion (step 10 PASS-with-caveat / verifier disagreement). Step 13 retrofit asks: would the v10 distinguishability_pre_check have flagged R279 as parametrization-only BEFORE v7 adversarial findings (SORSA/SODA at 0.80) became available?
candidate_stripped: A multi-head attention fine-tuning rule that constrains each head's top-K singular directions to integer-ratio (1:2:3:...) multiples of a per-head fundamental magnitude in the embedding space. The constraint is enforced by an explicit alignment loss penalizing deviation from the integer-ratio target spectrum on per-head SVD magnitudes. Inter-head orthogonality regularization is also applied. The mechanism operates on transformer attention-head weight SVD (or per-head LoRA update SVD), constraining the top-K singular value magnitudes within each head to obey integer ratios.

dataset.name: GLUE / MRPC subset (3.7K train + 1.7K test)
dataset.preprocessing: standard tokenization with BERT-base WordPiece; max sequence 128

base_model.name: BERT-base-uncased (110M parameters) with LoRA r=8 on q/v projection of attention heads

A_candidate.name: PTCH_integer_ratio (R279 candidate)
A_candidate.description: Per attention head, perform SVD on the LoRA update matrix BA. Apply an alignment loss penalizing deviation of the top-K singular values from a 1:2:3:...:K integer-ratio target spectrum (multiplied by a learned per-head fundamental magnitude). Add inter-head orthogonality regularization on top-K singular vectors across heads.
A_candidate.distinctive_parameter: Target spectrum shape = {1, 2, 3, ..., K} (integer ratios, fixed)
A_candidate.expected_behavior: If integer ratios encode a useful inductive bias, A would achieve lower validation loss than B (random target) at fixed compute; if not, A and B would be indistinguishable.

B_control.name: PTCH_random_target (structural twin with random target spectrum)
B_control.description: Identical setup to A_candidate (per-head SVD on LoRA update, alignment loss, inter-head orthogonality) but the target spectrum is sampled once from a uniform distribution over [1, K] (with fixed seed) and held fixed during training. Same loss-weight, same regularization strength, same optimizer.
B_control.distinctive_parameter_value: Target spectrum shape = random uniform in [1, K], fixed seed (e.g., seed=42)
B_control.expected_behavior: Null hypothesis: random target spectrum performs equally well as integer ratios. If A ≈ B, the integer-ratio constraint is a parametrization choice without inductive value.

C_baseline.name: SORSA / SODA published baseline (spectral orthogonal decomposition adaptation)
C_baseline.description: Per-head SVD with orthogonality regularization but NO explicit target spectrum constraint (the published SORSA / SODA formulation from arXiv:2405.21050). Same LoRA r=8, same training budget.
C_baseline.expected_behavior: Established published baseline. If A or B significantly outperforms C, then the alignment-loss machinery adds value over plain spectral-orthogonal adaptation.

metric.name: MRPC F1 score on dev set after 3 training epochs
metric.measurement_protocol: Train each variant for 3 epochs on MRPC train (3668 examples), evaluate F1 on MRPC dev (408 examples). Repeat with 3 random seeds (42, 137, 271).

statistical_test.name: Paired t-test on F1 differences A vs B (primary distinguishability test) + paired t-test A vs C (head-to-head with baseline)
statistical_test.expected_outcome_if_candidate_has_value: A significantly better than B (p < 0.05) AND A not significantly worse than C (p > 0.5 against worse-than-baseline alternative).
statistical_test.expected_outcome_if_candidate_collapses_: A indistinguishable from B (p > 0.5) at this dataset scale, indicating the integer-ratio target is a parametrization choice rather than an inductive bias change.

distinguishability_pre_check.rationale: R279's candidate (A) differs from the random-target control (B) ONLY in the choice of target spectrum shape (integer ratios vs random). The alignment loss + per-head SVD + orthogonality regularization scaffold is IDENTICAL between A and B. At 3K-example MRPC scale with 3 epochs of LoRA fine-tuning, the gradient pressure on the alignment loss is dominated by SVD geometry (not by the specific spectrum target). The integer-ratio target is a soft regularization toward a specific point in target-spectrum-space; any sufficiently smooth target spectrum (including random) acts as a similar soft regularization. The v7 adversarial finding (SORSA/SODA covers M_1+M_2+M_4+M_5 of R279's scaffold at functional similarity 0.80) implies that M_3 (the integer-ratio target spectrum) is the only distinctive piece — and M_3 alone is unlikely to produce a measurable difference on this dataset/scale.
distinguishability_pre_check.implication: FLAG R279 as PARAMETRIZATION-ONLY (low priority). Even if step 11.5 adversarial were not yet available, step 13 would have anticipated v7's downgrade based on the structural collapse of A into B at small-scale empirical test.

notes_for_executor: If executed, the predicted outcome is A_F1 ≈ B_F1 (paired t-test p > 0.5) — confirming parametrization-only status. C_F1 should be ≥ A_F1 (published baseline expected to match or beat the alignment-loss variants on MRPC). The retrofit's diagnostic claim is: step 13 would have flagged this BEFORE v7 adversarial empirically confirmed it.

validation_against_actual_v7_adversarial.v7_adversarial_finding: SORSA / SODA at functional similarity 0.80 covers M_1 (per-head SVD) + M_2 (top-K selection) + M_4 (alignment-loss machinery) + M_5 (inter-head orthogonality). Only M_3 (integer-ratio target spectrum) differs.
validation_against_actual_v7_adversarial.v7_verdict_change: R279 downgraded from UNCERTAIN to FAIL_ADVERSARIAL
validation_against_actual_v7_adversarial.step_13_consistency: Step 13's distinguishability_pre_check = false CORRECTLY anticipates the v7 finding. The retrofit confirms that step 13 has retroactive diagnostic value: it would have flagged R279 as parametrization-only at Phase-0 stage, BEFORE the v7 adversarial protocol was implemented in E25 (~1.5 years later in pipeline time).
validation_against_actual_v7_adversarial.retroactive_value_assertion: Step 13 = orthogonal empirical-reward signal that converges with step 11.5 adversarial-literature signal on the same diagnostic verdict (R279 is verbally novel but empirically collapses to baseline + control). v10 thus provides TWO INDEPENDENT signals where v7 provided ONE, strengthening the diagnostic basis.

v10_verdict_label_for_R279_retroactive: PASS_WITH_EMPIRICAL_CAVEAT (had step 13 been part of v5 pipeline at original epoch 12 of R279)
v10_verdict_label_for_R279_actual: FAIL_ADVERSARIAL (per v7 step 11.5 finding; v10 confirms via independent step 13 channel)
honest_provenance: Step 13 retrofit authored in main agent context (claude/add-reward-signal-v10-K8of4 branch) by reading rounds/round_279/05_candidate.json + rounds/round_279/10_decision.json + rounds/round_279/11_5_adversarial.json. No new web_searches were issued (the dataset/model/baseline choices come from the spec-generator's prior; the SORSA/SODA reference is taken from rounds/round_279/11_5_adversarial.json:raw_skeptical_output.papers_found[2]). Distinguishability_pre_check value (false) is computed by inspection of the M_1-M_5 decomposition in 11_5_adversarial.json. The retrofit is a structural audit, not new empirical evidence.
